# Workforce / labour scope template — Carney delivery (I-tpl-008).
#
# Used when the user is researching workforce, labour-market,
# employment, skills, automation displacement, immigration policy
# affecting labour, or productivity questions. The corpus should be
# dominated by labour-statistical agency outputs (T3) plus
# peer-reviewed labour economics research (T1) and policy-institute
# commentary (T6).

domain: workforce
description: >
  Workforce and labour-market corpus for employment trends, skills
  policy, automation displacement, and labour-economics research.
  Statistical-agency outputs and peer-reviewed labour economics are
  the primary evidence; advocacy-group reports require explicit
  funding-disclosure provenance.

inclusion_criteria:
  - "Statistics Canada (StatCan) Labour Force Survey, Survey of Employment Payrolls and Hours, Census long-form."
  - "US Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) — Current Employment Statistics, Current Population Survey, Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey, Productivity and Costs."
  - "OECD Employment Outlook, OECD Skills Outlook, OECD Future of Work."
  - "International Labour Organization (ILO) reports + ILOSTAT."
  - "Eurostat labour-market statistics."
  - "Peer-reviewed labour economics journals — Quarterly Journal of Economics, Journal of Labor Economics, Labour Economics, Industrial Labor Relations Review."
  - "Government-of-Canada Employment and Social Development Canada (ESDC) outputs + provincial labour-market analytics."
  - "Policy-institute reports — Brookings Hamilton Project, Aspen Institute Future of Work, Pew Research Center labour, RBC Climate Institute Future of Work."
  - "World Bank labour-market reports for international comparisons."

exclusion_criteria:
  - "Industry-association studies presented as primary research (allowed only as T6 with funding disclosure)."
  - "Advocacy-group reports without methodology section."
  - "Recruiting / staffing-agency surveys presented as labour-market data (allowed only as T5 vendor tier)."
  - "Op-eds in business media without disclosed analyst affiliation."

expected_tier_distribution:
  - tier: T3
    min_fraction: 0.35
    max_fraction: 0.65
    rationale: >
      Statistical-agency outputs (StatCan, BLS, OECD, ILO, Eurostat)
      are the primary quantitative evidence. Required for any claim
      involving labour-market metrics.
  - tier: T1
    min_fraction: 0.10
    max_fraction: 0.30
    rationale: >
      Peer-reviewed labour economics research papers.
  - tier: T6
    min_fraction: 0.10
    max_fraction: 0.25
    rationale: >
      Policy-institute reports — Brookings Hamilton Project, Aspen,
      Pew, RBC Climate Institute. Analyst tier, NOT peer-reviewed.
  - tier: T2
    min_fraction: 0.05
    max_fraction: 0.20
    rationale: >
      Systematic reviews / meta-analyses of labour-market literature.
  - tier: T5
    min_fraction: 0.00
    max_fraction: 0.10
    rationale: >
      Industry / staffing-agency surveys. Treat with skepticism;
      cite for technical-trade claims only.
  - tier: T4
    min_fraction: 0.00
    max_fraction: 0.10
    rationale: >
      Narrative reviews of workforce trends.

required_jurisdictions:
  - CA
  - US
  - OECD  # cross-country comparisons via OECD aggregates

  - statistical_agency_citation_required_for_quantitative_claims

# Allowed inputs used (NATIVE only): the locked question text ("a
# literature review on the restructuring impact of AI on the labor
# market ... AI as a key driver of the Fourth Industrial Revolution ...
# only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles"); this
# workforce scope_template (inclusion criteria naming QJE / Journal of
# Labor Economics / Labour Economics / AER-tier journals); the
# tracked, gold-blind `amplified` retrieval set already committed in
# scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py for drb_72_ai_labor (Autor;

# Manning-Salomons); and general labor-economics domain knowledge of the
# canonical peer-reviewed papers a faithful review of this literature
# MUST cite. Naming canonical papers is convergent validity, NOT gold
# leakage — the diff-time verbatim-overlap check is the contamination
# guard.
#
# Severity rationale (NATIVE): no S0. Every D8 s0_must_cover category is
# clinical (contraindications / dosing / black-box / pregnancy-renal-
# hepatic / regulatory-status); the workforce domain defines NO must-
# cover catastrophic/invalidating omission category, so per the design
# ruling severities are S1/S2/S3 only. The foundational
# automation/displacement econometric papers that any faithful review
# MUST cite to be valid -> S1; the supporting exposure/measurement and
# Fourth-Industrial-Revolution framing papers -> S2. The canonical id is
# `doi` where the journal article exposes one (exact-equality coverage:
# M3b regex-extracts the bare DOI token), with `url_pattern` reserved for
# the journal landing page where a DOI is the native citation anchor.
